statement.markdown: You are given a list of costs to collect each fruit from a garden. Your task is to determine the minimum cost of collecting all the fruits.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of two lines: the first line contains the total number of fruits, and the second line contains the costs of each fruit separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a single line containing the minimum cost to collect all the fruits.
statement.notes: You can collect fruits in any order, but you must collect them.

examples.explanation: You should collect the fruits in the order 4, 2, 1 to minimize the total cost.

hints.text: Check if sorting the fruits by cost is the best approach.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.betterSolution: Sorting the fruits by cost in descending order and collecting them in that order is the best approach.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& costs); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] costs); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, costs: list[int]) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& costs) { sort(costs.rbegin(), costs.rend()); int ans = 0; for (int i = 1; i < costs.size(); i++) ans += costs[i]; return ans; }; };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] costs) { Arrays.sort(costs); int ans = 0; for (int i = 1; i < costs.length; i++) ans += costs[i]; return ans; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, costs: list[int]) -> int: costs.sort(reverse=True); ans = 0; for i in range(1, len(costs)): ans += costs[i]; return ans;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(costs) { costs.sort((a, b) => b - a); let ans = 0; for (let i = 1; i < costs.length; i++) ans += costs[i]; return ans;
